one_time_description: Treaty loan forgiveness - $2,810,604 in comprehensive land claim negotiation debt owed to Canada was forgiven effective March 31, 2020
economic_notes: First Nation operates forestry enterprises (Tseshaht Forestry Corporation, Omoah Forestry LLP, Cisaa Forestry LLP), marine operations (Ekoolthaht Marine LLP), fisheries (Tseshaht Fisheries Corporation, 1055423 Fisheries LP), Equis Forest Products Ltd. (51%), Tseshaht Market (gas/convenience), Orange Bridge Cannabis (new department). COVID-19 noted as significant event impacting operations.
notes: Fiscal year is 2020 (not 2024). Five reporting segments: Operating, Trust, Social Housing, Enterprise, Treaty. Seven entities accounted for by modified equity method (GBEs). Consolidated subsidiaries include Orange Bridge Cannabis (new cannabis department), Social Housing, Tseshaht Market, Tseshaht Somass Society, Tseshaht Development Society, Tseshaht Economic Development Corporation. Major one-time item: $2,810,604 treaty loan forgiveness. Net financial assets of $5,791,965 includes significant jump due to loan forgiveness. Tangible capital assets net book value not extractable from text provided (Schedule 1 referenced but not included). Government transfers calculation: FNHA $405,062 + Province of BC $313,265 + CMHC $270,255 + Government of Canada $255,715 + FNESS $108,594 + NTC-CFNFA $3,850,629 + NTC non-CFNFA $1,362,235 + Nuu-chah-nulth Employment & Training Board $69,794 + Other grants $72,084 + BC Gaming $533,778 = partial; OSR set accordingly. COVID-19 noted as significant post-year event. No remuneration schedule included in extracted text.
